query: Boeing Q4S mission space-based quantum network entanglement swapping lab test details

mako.raw:
{"query":"Boeing Q4S mission space-based quantum network entanglement swapping lab test details","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1655,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://thequantuminsider.com/2026/06/18/boeing-lab-tests-mark-major-step-toward-space-based-quantum-network/","title":"Boeing Lab Tests Mark Major Step Toward Space-Based Quantum Network","content":"Boeing reported that its Q4S satellite payload successfully demonstrated high-fidelity entanglement swapping during ground testing, marking a step toward space-based quantum networking.
Boeing said the mission is intended to validate quantum networking technologies that could support future secure communications, precision timing, distributed sensing and a global quantum internet.
This result marks a major step toward proving quantum networking in orbit.
The team also completed environmental qualification testing, a series of tests designed to verify that the flight payload can withstand launch stresses and the harsh conditions of space.
In the nearer term, the underlying technologies could help support future secure communications architectures, more precise timing for navigation and distributed systems, validation of network integrity, and advanced sensing concepts that combine data across air, ground, sea and space.
Q4S is a key step in moving quantum networking toward practical aerospace and defense applications."},{"url":"https://quantumcomputingreport.com/boeing-advances-its-space-based-quantum-networking-project-with-successful-q4s-lab-tests/","title":"Boeing Advances Its Space-Based Quantum Networking Project with Successful Q4S Lab Tests - Quantum Computing Report","content":"Q4S is a planned one-year, on-orbit demonstration mission designed to test quantum networking hardware in the harsh environment of space."},{"url":"https://spacenews.com/boeing-demonstrates-quantum-protocol-in-payload-set-for-2027-launch/","title":"Boeing demonstrates quantum protocol in payload set for 2027 launch - SpaceNews","content":"Home / Boeing demonstrates quantum protocol in payload set for 2027 launch
PORTLAND, Ore. – Boeing demonstrated a key quantum networking protocol in ground testing of a compact payload ahead of on-orbit experiment in 2027.
“High-fidelity entanglement swapping” was demonstrated earlier this year by Boeing’s Q4S quantum networking satellite system, Boeing announced June 18.
“Quantum networking has the potential to transform how information is shared, timed and protected across global systems, but only if it can work outside the lab, under real mission constraints,” Lane Ballard, Boeing chief technology officer, said in a statement.
Boeing is paying for Q4S with independent research and development funding.
At that time, NASA, the University of Illinois and Boeing showed that a payload in orbit could generate pairs of photons that remained entangled in microgravity."},{"url":"https://payloadspace.com/boeing-to-launch-groundbreaking-orbital-quantum-experiment/","title":"Boeing To Launch Groundbreaking Orbital Quantum Experiment","content":"In 2017, Chinese scientists used a satellite to demonstrate entanglement between two ground stations 1,200 km apart.
Space-Grade Quantum: Quantum entanglement swapping has been demonstrated in the lab, but never before in space—and that’s where researchers think the best environment for long-range transmission will be.
Boeing’s Q4S will need to maintain precisely controlled internal temperatures and ensure that its mirrors don’t become misaligned during launch."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/keith-king-03a172128_boeings-quantum-leap-space-based-communications-activity-7329172250463494144-yLgP","title":"Boeing’s Quantum Leap: Space-Based Communications Set to Redefine Security | Keith King","content":"A Region Once Devastated By Disaster Is Now Building The Internet Of The Future. ⚡ Researchers at Tohoku Univ. have developed a photonic router that can direct quantum information with 99% fidelity and just 1.3% signal loss, a first-of-its-kind device capable of routing entangled photons while remaining compatible with today’s telecom networks."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Boeing Q4S mission space-based quantum network entanglement swapping lab test details","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://quantumcomputingreport.com/boeing-advances-its-space-based-quantum-networking-project-with-successful-q4s-lab-tests","title":"Boeing Advances Its Space-Based Quantum Networking Project with Successful Q4S Lab Tests - Quantum Computing Report","content":"# Boeing Advances Its Space-Based Quantum Networking Project with Successful Q4S Lab Tests. We had previously reported on a Boeing Q4S project to demonstrate entanglement swapping in space (see here and here). Now, Boeing has announced further progress with the completion of ground testing of the high-fidelity entanglement swapping in the compact, space-qualified Q4S payload. The payload has previously passed environmental qualification and is now undergoing final spacecraft integration ahead of a planned 2027 launch. Q4S is a planned one-year, on-orbit demonstration mission designed to test quantum networking hardware in the harsh environment of space. Jay Lowell, Chief Scientist for Boeing’s Quantum Systems organization commented, “One of the hardest parts of quantum networking is maintaining strong performance while working within the size, weight and power limits of a spacecraft. Following its 2027 launch, Boeing intends to collect one year of on-orbit performance data and submit the technical results for peer-reviewed publication.","score":0.9067276,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://boeing.mediaroom.com/news-releases-statements?item=131679","title":"News Releases | Boeing Newsroom","content":"- Boeing’s Q4S mission demonstrates leading entanglement swapping capability within the power, weight and environmental constraints of a compact satellite payload. - The milestone supports Boeing’s long-term vision to enable a global quantum internet connecting quantum sensors and quantum computers. - The flight payload has completed environmental qualification testing and is now in final spacecraft integration ahead of a planned 2027 launch and on-orbit demonstration. Boeing team members inspect the Q4S flight payload during testing designed to verify the hardware can withstand launch and on-orbit conditions ahead of its planned 2027 mission (Boeing image). Based on Boeing’s analysis of peer-reviewed publications of comparable entanglement-swapping experiments, the payload demonstrated leading performance within the real-world power and weight constraints of spaceflight. The team also completed environmental qualification testing, a series of tests designed to verify that the flight payload can withstand launch stresses and the harsh conditions of space. With the commencement of final spacecraft integration, the Q4S mission remains on track for a 2027 launch and on-orbit demonstration.","score":0.82391036,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.boeing.com/space/quantum","title":"Quantum Technology","content":"A quantum entanglement swapping experiment in space to lay the groundwork for a global quantum internet. Boeing is making a big bet on quantum, launching a self-funded quantum entanglement swapping demonstration satellite. The Q4S mission – slated for launch in 2027 – highlights Boeing’s desire to push the boundaries of quantum technology and its applications. A satellite orbiting Earth with the sun rising in the background, showcasing its advanced technology and design. Boeing is doing much more than participating in quantum research, we are leading the way to operationalize and scale quantum technologies for global applications. By taking on the challenge of entanglement swapping in space, Boeing is setting the stage for a future where quantum technology enhances how we connect, protect and explore the world and beyond. ##### Quantum entanglement swapping underpins communication of the future and we’re launching Q4S to prove it. Future systems will leverage quantum information from a sensor in space that can be delivered via entanglement swapping to a quantum computer on the ground.","score":0.7999589,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/quantum-spectator_boeing-has-announced-that-its-q4s-quantum-activity-7473590218927939584-dO9e","title":"Quantum Spectator's Post","content":"Boeing has announced that its Q4S quantum networking satellite system successfully demonstrated high-fidelity entanglement swapping during","score":0.72884524,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://spacesecurity.wse.jhu.edu/2024/09/30/boeing-entanglement-swapping-test-is-critical-for-quantum-network","title":"Boeing Entanglement-Swapping Test is Critical for ...","content":"Sep 30, 2024 — Boeing hopes to launch the Q4S satellite in 2026 “to demonstrate quantum entanglement swapping, a process in which two pairs of quanta- in ...Read more","score":0.7161595,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.69,"request_id":"c92d61a8-d739-4fcf-bca6-d5f4f29c337b"}
